tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, indicating impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, suggesting possible nephropathy. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor, low specific gravity) further support renal involvement. Mild glycosuria is present despite normal blood glucose and low HbA1c, possibly due to renal threshold changes. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal function and proteinuria.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
